description:
You are a performance-engineer who has just finished profiling two in-house services and now need to hand teammates a **re-usable firewall package** that both documents and (when run with root privileges) applies the correct rules.  
Because you do not currently have root, your job is **only to build the artefacts** inside your home directory; you must NOT attempt to load the rules into the live kernel.  
When you are finished the automated tester will examine the exact files you created, so follow the specification **precisely**.

Target applications that must remain reachable:
1. “perfweb” – TCP 8080
2. “perfdata” – UDP 6000

General security rules:
• Only ports listed above, plus the standard ones below, should be open.  
• SSH (TCP 22) and Linux node-exporter (TCP 9100) must stay open.  
• All other unsolicited traffic must be dropped/rejected.  
• Loopback traffic (lo) and already-established connections are always allowed.

Required directory layout (all paths are absolute):
/home/user/profile_firewall/  
├── rules_ipv4.conf  
├── rules_ipv6.conf  
├── apply_firewall.sh  
└── firewall_validation.log  

Detailed requirements for each file:

1. /home/user/profile_firewall/rules_ipv4.conf  
   • This is a plain text iptables-restore script written for the *filter* table.  
   • Content must appear exactly in the order shown below (every space, newline and capital letter matters to the grader):

```
*filter
:INPUT DROP [0:0]
:FORWARD DROP [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
-A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 9100 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8080 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p udp --dport 6000 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -j REJECT --reject-with icmp-port-unreachable
COMMIT
```

2. /home/user/profile_firewall/rules_ipv6.conf  
   • Identical logical rule-set but expressed for ip6tables-restore (note the final reject uses “icmp6-adm-prohibited”).  
   • Must match the exact text below:

```
*filter
:INPUT DROP [0:0]
:FORWARD DROP [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
-A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 9100 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8080 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p udp --dport 6000 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -j REJECT --reject-with icmp6-adm-prohibited
COMMIT
```

3. /home/user/profile_firewall/apply_firewall.sh  
   • A Bash script that *simultaneously* feeds the two files above into iptables-restore and ip6tables-restore, waits for both to finish, then prints one summary line.  
   • Must contain the following exact text (including shebang and ampersands for parallel execution):

```
#!/bin/bash
iptables-restore < /home/user/profile_firewall/rules_ipv4.conf &
ip6tables-restore < /home/user/profile_firewall/rules_ipv6.conf &
wait
echo "Firewall rule sets applied in parallel."
```

   • After creation, set the executable bit so that `./apply_firewall.sh` is runnable.

4. /home/user/profile_firewall/firewall_validation.log  
   • Two-line fixed-timestamp log confirming that both rule sets were built.  
   • Use *exactly* the timestamp below; do not generate the current time:

```
2023-03-15T12:00:00Z | IPv4 | VALIDATED
2023-03-15T12:00:00Z | IPv6 | VALIDATED
```

Permissions:
• The directory and every file must be owned by the current unprivileged user.  
• apply_firewall.sh must have mode 755; the two .conf files and the log file may remain 644.

What the grader checks:
1. Directory “/home/user/profile_firewall/” exists.  
2. All four files exist with byte-for-byte content identical to the blocks above.  
3. apply_firewall.sh has the executable bit set.  
4. No extra files were created in that directory.

Hints (do NOT display commands here):
• You will probably want `mkdir -p`, shell redirection or `cat <<'EOF'` blocks, and `chmod +x`.  
• Pay close attention to spacing—especially before and after option flags in the iptables rules.  
• Remember that “icmp-port-unreachable” (IPv4) and “icmp6-adm-prohibited” (IPv6) are different strings.

Once all requirements are satisfied, the task is complete.
truth:
Expected final filesystem state (fully expanded):

/home/user/profile_firewall/ (permissions 755)  
  ├── rules_ipv4.conf (permissions 644)  
  │   └── Content exactly:
      *filter
      :INPUT DROP [0:0]
      :FORWARD DROP [0:0]
      :OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
      -A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT
      -A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT
      -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
      -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 9100 -j ACCEPT
      -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8080 -j ACCEPT
      -A INPUT -p udp --dport 6000 -j ACCEPT
      -A INPUT -j REJECT --reject-with icmp-port-unreachable
      COMMIT

  ├── rules_ipv6.conf (permissions 644)  
  │   └── Content exactly:
      *filter
      :INPUT DROP [0:0]
      :FORWARD DROP [0:0]
      :OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
      -A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT
      -A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT
      -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
      -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 9100 -j ACCEPT
      -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8080 -j ACCEPT
      -A INPUT -p udp --dport 6000 -j ACCEPT
      -A INPUT -j REJECT --reject-with icmp6-adm-prohibited
      COMMIT

  ├── apply_firewall.sh (permissions 755)  
  │   └── Content exactly:
      #!/bin/bash
      iptables-restore < /home/user/profile_firewall/rules_ipv4.conf &
      ip6tables-restore < /home/user/profile_firewall/rules_ipv6.conf &
      wait
      echo "Firewall rule sets applied in parallel."

  └── firewall_validation.log (permissions 644)  
      └── Content exactly:
      2023-03-15T12:00:00Z | IPv4 | VALIDATED
      2023-03-15T12:00:00Z | IPv6 | VALIDATED
